title: Beef & Broccoli with Rice
description: A delicious and easy-to-make beef and broccoli stir-fry served with fluffy rice, perfect for a family dinner. This recipe is designed to be quick and uses minimal pans for easy cleanup.
instructions: Slice the flank steak thinly against the grain.
In a bowl, whisk together soy sauce, brown sugar, grated ginger, and minced garlic to create the sauce.
In a small cup, mix cornstarch with water to create a slurry.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced beef to the skillet and stir-fry until browned, about 3-4 minutes. Remove beef and set aside.
Add broccoli florets to the same skillet and stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until tender-crisp.
Pour the sauce into the skillet with the broccoli. Bring to a simmer.
Stir in the cornstarch slurry and cook until the sauce thickens.
Return the cooked beef to the skillet and toss to coat with the sauce.
tips: For extra flavor, marinate the beef in a tablespoon of soy sauce and a pinch of baking soda for 15 minutes before cooking.
If your kids are picky about broccoli, you can steam it separately until very tender before adding it to the stir-fry.
Adjust the amount of brown sugar to your family's preference for sweetness in the sauce.
